name: Fisherman's Bread (Pane Del Pescatore)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine butter and sugar in a large mixing bowl. Beat until fluffy, light, and pale in color, about 5-7 minutes on medium speed of an electric mixer.
2. Add whole egg and continue to beat, scraping the sides of the bowl. Add yolk and beat well, scraping the sides of the bowl again.
3. In a separate mixing bowl, stir together flour, baking powder, raisins, candied orange and lemon peels, and fennel seeds.
4. Add half of the flour mixture to the butter mixture and mix until dry ingredients are thoroughly incorporated.
5. Beat in 2 tablespoons milk and Marsala. Add remaining flour mixture and continue to mix just until a rough, shaggy dough forms.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board and knead with lightly floured hands until the dough is soft but still slightly sticky, about 2 minutes.
7. Divide the dough into 2 equal portions. Shape each portion into a ball, then using the palms of your hands, flatten the balls into disks about 1 inch thick.
8. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or grease with butter. Place the shaped loaves on the baking sheet.
9. With a small serrated knife, cut a simple shallow grid pattern in the top of each loaf. Brush the tops with the remaining 1 tablespoon milk.
10. Bake the loaves at 350°F until light golden-brown and a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out dry, about 35-40 minutes. Remove to wire racks to cool completely. Makes 2 (6") loaves, about 8 servings.
Each serving contains about: 399 calories; 143 milligrams sodium; 107 milligrams cholesterol; 19 grams fat; 50 grams carbohydrates; 6 grams protein; 0.54 gram fiber.
